artifacts.name: Post Saint: ChatGPT, GPT4, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Pexels, Dezgo AI Text & Image Generator

description.value: Post Saint: ChatGPT, GPT4, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Pexels, Dezgo AI Text & Image Generator <= 1.3.1 - Missing Authorization to Authenticated (Subscriber+) Arbitrary File Upload (CVE-2024-12471)

The Post Saint: ChatGPT, GPT4, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Pexels, Dezgo AI Text & Image Generator plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary files uploads due to a missing capability check and file type validation on the add_image_to_library AJAX action function in all versions up to, and including, 1.3.1. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to upload arbitrary files that make remote code execution possible.

Reason for inclusion in AVID: The CVE describes an authenticated arbitrary file upload vulnerability (leading to remote code execution) in a WordPress plugin that integrates AI tools (ChatGPT, GPT-4, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion). This plugin is used to deploy AI-generated content, making it a component within AI software stacks. The vulnerability affects a software supply chain component used to build/run AI features on websites, and constitutes a security vulnerability with clear exploitation potential.

cwe.description: CWE-94 Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
